pages.raw_text:
Special Correspondent to The Press.


CHICAGO, May 1S.-"Another
dirty plot of the PinkertOns.' is
what union labor men in this city
say of the story sprung here a few
days ago which aims to connect
Charles II Meyer, the miners presi-
dent, under accusation in Boise,
with the Charles II. Meyer who was
sent to Joliet penitentiary from
Chicago in 1886 for burglary.


The story is base fabrication
intended to bias judgment at the
moment the Haywood trial is begin
ming say Meyers friends,


The Chicago Meyer of p years
ago operated with John Heating, an
is year old boy, in a series of dar-
ing holdups and robberies in the
latter part of 1885 Among his vic
Tins were several millionaires. He
and his pal were captured Dec. 4.
1885, after a battle with the po-
licemen, in which Meyer was beat
en- into insensibility.


The missing link in the endeav-
or to connect the two Meyers is
Sam Williams, who owned the
stable where Meyer and Heating
made their rendezvous. Williams
left Chicago years ago, but the
Pinkertons claim he has been To-
Gated and will identify Mover.
